response.justification: Bogdan is a strong, broad-skilled individual contributor with nearly 20 years of engineering experience and genuine developer tooling exposure (test frameworks, CI/CD infrastructure, MCP tooling), but this role is fundamentally a people management position. There is zero evidence of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, or owning a team's organizational roadmap anywhere in his career history. The 2-year people management minimum is a hard requirement, and his trajectory — Staff/Senior IC, founder, conference speaker — reflects technical leadership by influence rather than formal management. He is not a fit for this role as written.
strengths: Hands-on developer tooling experience across multiple roles: built CI/CD infrastructure at Altom, authored Fluent-Asserts and the Trial test runner, and currently contributes to MCP code generators and agentic frameworks at re:cinq
Nearly 20 years of broad engineering depth across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency systems — well above the 8-year minimum
Consistent knowledge-sharing through workshops, Refactoring Dojos, and conference talks, demonstrating the coaching instinct relevant to team development
concerns: No people management experience anywhere in the resume — the role requires 2+ years managing direct reports, which is a non-negotiable baseline
No evidence of setting team priorities, defining mid-term technical roadmaps at an organizational level, or driving performance management processes
Mobile platform ecosystem depth is shallow — one brief iOS/Apple TV engagement at WatchUp; the role centers on mobile platform engineering productivity at scale

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and test infrastructure background
No organizational leadership or strategic roadmap ownership
